hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: You raised before the cards were dealt. Your opponent called.
Flop — King, King, King. You bet. Your opponent raised you back.

wrongReasons: 6 Q plays similarly, but the correct hand pairs a specific board card that 6 Q doesn't. Look at which hole card ranks actually appear on the board.
K 7 makes a four of a kind here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
9 3 plays similarly, but the correct hand pairs a specific board card that 9 3 doesn't. Look at which hole card ranks actually appear on the board.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 6 10

Your opponent flopped three of a kind and raised the flop immediately to build the pot. Sets are too strong to slow-play — they pushed their advantage on every street.
